subject: Anthropology
title: Microfinance
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Microfinance.

hook.text: Tiny loans to poor women were supposed to end poverty. Microfinance won a Nobel Prize. But did it actually work?

buildup.text: Microfinance provides small loans to people excluded from formal banking, especially women in developing countries.

discovery.text: Muhammad Yunus and Grameen Bank showed that the poor are creditworthy. Repayment rates often exceeded traditional banks.

twist.text: Critics found that microfinance often traps borrowers in debt. It can empower individuals but rarely ends poverty.

climax.text: Microfinance reveals a tension: individual solutions can't fix structural problems. Credit without justice isn't enough.

punchline.text: Small loans can't fix big systems.

quiz.question: What is a major critique of microfinance?
options: It can trap borrowers in debt without addressing structural poverty
